data.title: Kilde receives $1.5M funding for growth, led by Purple Ventures. Shows confidence in platform's trajectory.
data.description: In Singapore on September 19, 2025, Kilde, a licensed private credit investment platform, successfully concluded a Pre-Series A financing round worth USD 1.5 million. Purple Ventures spearheaded the round with contributions from current investors, highlighting robust belief in Kilde's future expansion.

World Bank President Plans More Securitisation Sales FT
Goldman Sachs Now Predicts First Bank of England Rate Cut in February 2026 Instead of November 2025, with Quarterly Cuts Expected to Reach a 3% Terminal Rate by End of 2026

data.title: Brera Holdings rebrands to 'Solmate,' sees shares surge 225% with Solana Treasury strategy. Stock hits intraday high of $52.95.
data.description: Brera Holdings PLC (NASDAQ: BREA) experienced a significant surge in their shares during regular trading, with prices skyrocketing by triple digits. However, in after-hours trading on Thursday, the stock retraced by 11.05%, cooling down from its earlier substantial gains. Despite this pullback, BREA shares are still reaching new milestones as they hit an intraday high of $52.95. This peak signifies a remarkable 592% surge compared to the previous day's closing price. The surge was triggered by the Ireland-based company's rebranding to Solmate, emphasizing its focus on Solana (CRYPTO: SOL) in the cryptocurrency treasury sector. For more details on this development, readers can refer to the full article available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Trump considers new CFTC chair candidates as Quintenz's confirmation faces delay due to enforcement disagreements with Gemini.
data.description: President Trump is said to be considering alternative candidates for leading the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) following the Winklevoss twins obstructing Brian Quintenz's nomination due to disagreements over enforcement matters with the Gemini exchange.
BoE rate cut anticipated in December, says BNP Paribas, delaying prior November estimate.

data.title: Bybit partners with QNB Group and DMZ Finance to accept QCDT in Dubai, unlocking institutional access to digital assets.
data.description: In Dubai, UAE, on September 19, 2025, Bybit, the world's second-largest cryptocurrency exchange based on trading volume, revealed a strategic partnership with QNB Group and DMZ Finance. This collaboration aims to launch QCDT, the globe's first DFSA-approved (Dubai Financial Services Authority) cryptocurrency derivatives trading platform.

data.title: Huawei unveils AI-focused Xinghe Intelligent Network at HUAWEI CONNECT 2025, impacting industries. Leon Wang speaks at the event.
data.description: In Shanghai on September 18, 2025, Huawei organized the data communication innovation summit titled "Xinghe Intelligent Network: Shaping Intelligent Connectivity in the AI Era" as part of HUAWEI CONNECT 2025. Leon Wang, President of Huawei's Data Communication Product Line, was a key figure at the summit.
